title: 10 best herbal tinctures for upper abdominal pain
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for upper abdominal pain are concentrated liquid extracts made from plants, used to alleviate discomfort and reduce inflammation in the upper abdomen. These tinctures work by soothing the digestive system, relaxing the muscles, and reducing spasms that can cause upper abdominal pain. For example, peppermint tincture has natural analgesic properties that calm cramps and spasms, while ginger tincture helps to ease nausea and bloating. By using these herbal remedies, individuals can find relief from chronic discomfort and enjoy improved digestion, reduced stress, and a better overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with upper abdominal pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds, gingerols and shogaols, work to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating discomfort and cramping associated with conditions such as indigestion, bloating, and IBS. Additionally, ginger's warming properties help to stimulate digestion and relieve spasms in the muscles of the stomach, providing fast and effective relief from upper abdominal pain. Regular use can also help to maintain a healthy digestive system and prevent future episodes.
remedy_properties: Digestive Relief: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to soothe digestive issues like nausea, bloating, and cramps, reducing discomfort and pain in the upper abdomen.

Anti-Inflammatory Action: Ginger's bioactive compounds have potent anti-inflammatory properties that inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals, thereby reducing inflammation and associated pain in the abdominal region.

Gastric Protection: Ginger's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects help to protect the stomach lining from damage caused by acid reflux, gastritis, or other irritants, alleviating upper abdominal discomfort and pain.
remedy_constituents: Gingerol: A type of terpene, gingerol is a natural anti-inflammatory agent that helps reduce inflammation and alleviate upper abdominal pain.
Shogaol: A phenolic compound derived from ginger, shogaol has been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to relieve pain and discomfort in the upper abdomen.
6-Gingerol: A sesquiterpene, 6-gingerol has been found to have strong anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate upper abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: As they are closely related to rhizomes, roots are also rich in gingerols and shogaols, making them a popular choice for tincture production.

Buds: The buds of the ginger plant are used in traditional medicine due to their high concentration of active compounds, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the upper abdominal region.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the zingiber officinale roots in early morning after the dew has dried completely.
Wash the harvested roots thoroughly with distilled water and pat them dry with paper towels.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces using a sharp knife and measure out 500 grams of them.
Combine the chopped roots with 70% ethanol in a 1:5 ratio and let it steep for 2 weeks in a glass jar.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with upper abdominal pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. The curcumin present in turmeric tincture has potent antioxidant and analgesic effects that help alleviate symptoms such as cramps, bloating, and discomfort associated with upper abdominal pain. Additionally, turmeric tincture's natural antimicrobial properties may also aid in soothing irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other digestive disorders that can cause stomachaches and discomfort in the upper abdomen.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Turmeric tincture's active compound, curcumin, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the upper abdomen, alleviating pain.

Cyclooxygenase inhibition: Curcuminoids inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that contribute to pain and inflammation, providing relief from upper abdominal pain.

Gastrointestinal mucosal protection: Turmeric tincture's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties can help protect the gastrointestinal lining from damage, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with upper abdominal pain.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin is a polyphenolic compound that helps with upper abdominal pain by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes that contribute to pain and discomfort.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC is a curcuminoid with potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate upper abdominal pain by reducing oxidative stress and modulating the body's response to inflammation.

Turmerone (Ar-turmerone): Turmerone is a sesquiterpene that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to reduce upper abdominal pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting the healing of damaged tissues.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Curcuma longa for making tinctures due to their high concentration of curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Roots: Roots are another significant part used to make tinctures, as they contain a combination of curcuminoids and other bioactive compounds that help alleviate upper abdominal pain.

Leaves: Leaves are sometimes used to make tinctures, particularly in traditional medicine, as they are believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe upper abdominal discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 3-4 pounds of fresh curcuma longa rhizomes in late summer or early fall when the tops are dying back.
Cut the rhizomes into small pieces and dry them in a single layer at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine the dried rhizomes with 80 proof vodka at a ratio of 1:5 and allow the mixture to macerate for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids and press on the solids to extract remaining liquid.
Transfer the liquid to dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks to mature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with upper abdominal pain because it contains menthol, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that soothes the digestive tract. When taken in tincture form, peppermint oil is absorbed quickly into the bloodstream and reaches the stomach, where it calms cramps, spasms, and inflammation associated with conditions such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), acid reflux, and gastritis. This fast-acting relief helps to reduce pain, discomfort, and digestive distress, promoting a sense of calm and well-being in individuals experiencing upper abdominal pain.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Herbal peppermint tincture helps relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramping and spasms that can contribute to upper abdominal pain.
Antioxidant properties: Peppermint tincture contains antioxidants that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to upper abdominal pain.
Carminative properties: Herbal peppermint tincture has been shown to reduce gas and bloating, which are common causes of upper abdominal discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Helps with upper abdominal pain by reducing inflammation and relaxing the smooth muscle in the gastrointestinal tract, thus providing relief from cramps and spasms.
Limonene: Acts as a natural antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce muscle spasms and alleviate upper abdominal pain by modulating the gut-brain axis.

Rosmarinic acid: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gastrointestinal tract, thereby providing relief from upper abdominal pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part, as they contain the highest concentration of menthol, which helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and alleviate pain.
Buds: Buds are also used, as they contain a high concentration of menthone, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation and relieve pain in the upper abdomen.

Stems: Stems may also be used, as they contain a smaller amount of menthol and menthone than leaves and buds, but still have some analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to alleviate upper abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers at peak potency in the morning.
Chop 1 part fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers into small pieces using sharp tools.
Combine 1 part fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers with 2 parts 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks with occasional shaking to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with upper abdominal pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and relax the digestive tract. The volatile oils present in fennel, such as anethole, have a direct effect on the muscles lining the stomach and intestines, reducing spasms and cramping. Additionally, fennel's carminative properties help to expel gas and relieve bloating, which can contribute to upper abdominal discomfort. By addressing these underlying issues, herbal fennel tinctures provide effective relief from persistent upper abdominal pain.
remedy_properties: Carminative action: Fennel's essential oils, particularly anethole, have a carminative effect, which helps to alleviate bloating and cramps by reducing gas and improving digestion, thus relieving upper abdominal pain.
Antispasmodic properties: Fennel tincture contains compounds that relax smooth muscle tissues in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping that can contribute to upper abdominal pain.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Fennel's flavonoids and terpenes have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the abdominal area, alleviating pain and discomfort caused by conditions such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) or inflammatory bowel disease (IBD).
remedy_constituents: Anethole: Anethole, a terpene found in Foeniculum vulgare, acts as a smooth muscle relaxant, which helps to alleviate upper abdominal pain by reducing spasms and cramping in the digestive tract.

Feniculin: Feniculin, a phenolic compound in Foeniculum vulgare, has anti-inflammatory properties that may help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the upper abdominal region, contributing to pain relief.
Limonene: Limonene, a terpene present in Foeniculum vulgare, exhibits carminative properties, which help to relieve upper abdominal pain by reducing gas and bloating in the digestive system.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds are used due to their high concentration of volatile oils, particularly anethole, which has anti-inflammatory and carminative properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their ability to ease digestion and reduce inflammation, making them effective in alleviating upper abdominal pain.
Roots: The roots are used for their bitter properties, which can stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of upper abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of foeniculum vulgare roots and 500ml of 95% ethanol for extraction.
Chop the foeniculum vulgare roots into small pieces and combine them with ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow for extraction.
Strain the liquid from the solids using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Transfer the tincture to glass bottles and label them with the date and ingredients used for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with upper abdominal pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can soothe and calm the digestive system. The bioactive compounds in licorice root, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD), and other upper abdominal discomforts. By reducing inflammation and soothing the digestive tract, herbal licorice tinctures can provide quick and effective relief from upper abdominal pain.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the stomach lining, thereby alleviating pain associated with conditions like gastritis, ulcers, and gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD).

Flavonoids: Licorice tincture contains flavonoids, such as glabridin and isoliquiritigenin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe the stomach lining, protect against acid damage, and reduce pain associated with digestive disorders.

Saponins: Saponins in licorice tincture have mucilaginous properties that can help form a protective barrier on the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and irritation, which can help alleviate symptoms of heartburn, indigestion, and other upper abdominal pains.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and protect the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and pain associated with upper abdominal discomfort.

Flavonoids (including quercetin and kaempferol): These phenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help to relax the stomach muscles, reduce inflammation, and alleviate pain and discomfort in the upper abdomen.

Liquiritigenin: This isoflavone has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help to reduce inflammation, relax the stomach muscles, and alleviate pain and discomfort in the upper abdomen.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and can help soothe digestive issues.
Roots: Used due to their ability to calm the stomach and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract.
Roots: Used due to their adaptogenic properties, which can help the body cope with stress and alleviate upper abdominal pain caused by anxiety or stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots in late summer or early fall when the plant is in full bloom.
Clean and dry the harvested roots in a warm and well-ventilated area for 7 to 10 days.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and combine with 80 proof vodka in a 1:5 root-to-liquid ratio.
Steep the chopped roots in the vodka mixture for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with upper abdominal pain because it contains a rich source of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and bitter principles that stimulate digestive processes. The bitter constituents in dandelion tincture increase bile production, which helps to emulsify fats, break down proteins, and facilitate the elimination of waste products from the body. Additionally, dandelion's antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles in the abdominal wall, reducing cramping and discomfort associated with upper abdominal pain.
remedy_properties: Digestive Bitters: Dandelion tincture stimulates digestive juices to flow, helping to relieve bloating, gas, and cramps associated with upper abdominal pain.
Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: The antioxidants and flavonoids present in dandelion tincture help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to upper abdominal pain.
Choleretic Effects: Dandelion tincture stimulates the production of bile, which helps to break down fats and relieve tension in the gallbladder, thereby reducing upper abdominal pain.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: This triterpene ester helps with upper abdominal pain by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response, which can contribute to pain relief.
Luteolin: This flavonoid acts as an anti-inflammatory agent and can inhibit the production of pain-causing chemicals, such as prostaglandins, which contribute to upper abdominal pain.
Inulin: Although inulin itself is not a pain-relieving compound, it can help to stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation, which can be a contributing factor to upper abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Taraxacum officinale contain bitter compounds that stimulate digestion and relieve upper abdominal pain.
Roots: The roots of Taraxacum officinale contain sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and digestive-stimulating properties that help alleviate upper abdominal pain.
Buds: The buds of Taraxacum officinale contain compounds that help soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and alleviating upper abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in the early morning with clean scissors.
Dry the harvested taraxacum officinale in a single layer at 25°c for 7 to 10 days.
Combine 100g of dried taraxacum officinale with 500ml of 95% ethanol in a glass container.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 3 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tinctures helps with upper abdominal pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. The tincture's active compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and thymol, also exhibit analgesic and antispasmodic effects, providing relief from cramping and spasms that can contribute to upper abdominal discomfort. Additionally, sage has been shown to improve digestion and reduce symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), which may also help alleviate upper abdominal pain.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Sage tincture helps to relax muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to upper abdominal pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage tincture contains compounds that reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), acid reflux, and other conditions that cause upper abdominal pain.

Antioxidant properties: Sage tincture's antioxidant activity helps protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of gastrointestinal ulcers and inflammation that can contribute to upper abdominal pain.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A polyphenolic compound that helps reduce inflammation and relax smooth muscles in the digestive tract, alleviating cramps and spasms that contribute to upper abdominal pain.
Carnosic acid: A phenolic compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and protect the digestive system, reducing pain and discomfort in the upper abdomen.

Carnosol: A phenolic compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and promote the healing of the digestive tract, alleviating pain and discomfort in the upper abdomen.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of essential oils, particularly thujone and rosmarinic acid, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: The stems are also used, as they contain a significant amount of volatile oils, including camphor and borneol, which contribute to their pain-relieving and digestive-soothing effects.

Roots: The roots of Salvia officinalis are sometimes used, although less frequently, as they contain a lower concentration of essential oils than leaves and stems, but still possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that may aid in reducing upper abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250g of fresh salvia officinalis leaves and flowers and 500ml of 80 proof ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Combine the plant material and ethanol in the jar and seal it tightly for 2 weeks.
Shake the jar vigorously twice daily for 2 weeks to ensure proper extraction of the plant's properties.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for 4 weeks to allow the flavors to mature before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with upper abdominal pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The tannins present in the plant help to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles, providing relief from cramps and spasms that can cause upper abdominal pain. Additionally, the saponins in maidenhair tree have been shown to have a soothing effect on the digestive tract, helping to calm irritations and reduce discomfort in the abdominal area.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the stomach lining, which can contribute to upper abdominal pain.

Soothing anti-inflammatory effects: The tannins and flavonoids present in Maidenhair Tree tincture have anti-inflammatory properties that may help calm digestive issues, such as cramps, spasms, or irritability that cause upper abdominal discomfort.

Vasodilatory action: Ginkgo's ability to dilate blood vessels can improve circulation and reduce constriction in the stomach and intestines, which may alleviate cramping, bloating, and other symptoms of upper abdominal pain.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: This triterpene sesterterpene derivative has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate upper abdominal pain by reducing inflammation and discomfort in the digestive tract.

Quercetin: As a flavonoid, quercetin has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation in the upper abdominal region, leading to pain relief.

Icariin: This flavonoid glycoside has anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties, which can help improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the upper abdominal region, contributing to pain relief and relaxation of the digestive tract.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Ginkgo biloba leaves are commonly used due to their high flavonoid and terpenoid content, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate upper abdominal pain.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are used in tinctures due to their high content of ginkgotoxin, a compound that may help reduce inflammation and relieve upper abdominal pain.
Barks: Ginkgo biloba barks are sometimes used in tinctures due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help soothe and calm the upper abdominal region.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves in late summer or early fall when they are fully ripe for best flavor and potency.
Dry the leaves in a warm oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to remove excess moisture completely.
Combine 1 part dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow the infusion to mature fully.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids completely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese thoroughwort tinctures helps with upper abdominal pain because of its ability to calm digestive inflammation and relax smooth muscle spasms. The herb contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the abdominal region. Additionally, Chinese thoroughwort has a sedative effect on the muscles, allowing it to ease cramping and tension that often accompanies upper abdominal pain. remedy_properties: Stomach-soothing effect: The tannins and flavonoids in Zhī Zǐ help to calm stomach irritations, reduce inflammation, and ease discomfort associated with upper abdominal pain.

Carminative and anti-spasmodic properties: The essential oils and other compounds in the herb help to relieve gas, bloating, and cramps by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract and reducing spasms that can contribute to upper abdominal pain.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Zhī Zǐ's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the digestive system, which can contribute to chronic abdominal pain and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These triterpenoid compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate upper abdominal pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive tract.

Flavonoids (specifically, kaempferol and quercetin): These phenolic compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which can help mitigate upper abdominal pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive tract, as well as modulating the gut microbiome.

Buplurin: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate upper abdominal pain by reducing inflammation and pain in the digestive tract, as well as modulating the gut-brain axis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Bupleurum falcatum contain a high concentration of bitter compounds, which are believed to aid in digestion and relieve upper abdominal pain.
Leaves: The leaves of Bupleurum falcatum contain flavonoids and alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate upper abdominal pain and discomfort.

Stems: The stems of Bupleurum falcatum contain sesquiterpenes and alkaloids, which have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including upper abdominal pain, by promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh bupleurum falcatum roots and clean them thoroughly with water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop 1 part of the cleaned roots and combine with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks to allow the roots to infuse into the vodka.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Label the bottle and store it in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks before using the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica tinctures helps with upper abdominal pain because it has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The angelica plant, also known as wild celery, contains compounds that help to soothe and relax the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and spasms in the stomach and intestines. This can provide relief from cramps, bloating, and discomfort associated with upper abdominal pain, allowing for a faster recovery and improved overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Angelica tincture helps to relax smooth muscle spasms in the digestive tract, reducing cramping and discomfort associated with conditions such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and gallstones.

Anti-inflammatory property: The tincture's anti-inflammatory effects help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the abdominal region, which can contribute to pain and discomfort caused by conditions like appendicitis or pancreatitis.

Gastroprotective property: Angelica tincture has been shown to protect the stomach lining from damage caused by acid reflux, ulcers, and other digestive issues, helping to reduce nausea, vomiting, and upper abdominal pain.
remedy_constituents: Bergapten: A furanocoumarin found in Angelica archangelica, bergapten has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate upper abdominal pain.

Angelica acid: An isobutyrate-based phenolic acid found in the herb, angelica acid has been demonstrated to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, providing relief from upper abdominal pain.

Bornyl acetate: A terpene found in the essential oil of Angelica archangelica, bornyl acetate has been shown to have antispasmodic effects, helping to relax the muscles in the upper abdominal region and reduce pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their carminative and anti-inflammatory properties to soothe abdominal discomfort.
Leaves: Used for their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects to ease cramps and pain in the upper abdomen.
Stems: Used for their expectorant properties, which can help relieve digestive discomfort and gas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest angelica archangelica roots and rhizomes in late summer or early fall when they are mature and dry.
Dry the harvested roots and rhizomes in a warm place with good air circulation for 2-4 weeks.
Chop the dried roots and rhizomes into small pieces and combine them with 80-proof vodka in a ratio of 1 part plant material to 2 parts vodka.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar for 4-6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow gentian tinctures helps with upper abdominal pain because of its ability to stimulate digestion and reduce inflammation. The herb's bitter compounds help to increase gastric acid production, which aids in protein digestion and breaks down food more efficiently. This can alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramping, and discomfort associated with indigestion and irritable bowel syndrome. Anti-inflammatory property: Yellow gentian's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to pain and discomfort in the upper abdomen.

Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes contain alkaloids that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce swelling and discomfort in the upper abdomen.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gentiana lutea roots and clean them thoroughly under cold running water.
Cut the cleaned roots into small pieces and dry them in a warm, dry place for two weeks.
Grind the dried gentiana lutea roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one part of the powdered gentiana lutea with five parts of high-proof vodka and store the mixture in a dark glass bottle.
Allow the mixture to macerate for four weeks, shaking the bottle daily, before straining it through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with upper abdominal pain because its natural anti-inflammatory compounds, apigenin and bisabolol, work to soothe and calm the digestive system. The herb's gentle relaxing properties also help to reduce muscle spasms and cramping that can contribute to discomfort in the upper abdomen. Additionally, chamomile's antispasmodic effects can ease irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) symptoms, such as bloating and gas, which often accompany upper abdominal pain.

Soothing and calming effects: The apigenin and luteolin flavonoids in chamomile tincture have a sedative effect on the gut, which can help calm irritated or inflamed tissues, reducing spasms and pain associated with upper abdominal disorders such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).

Antispasmodic properties: Chamomile's antispasmodic compounds, including volatile oils and flavonoids, can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, which may ease cramps, spasms, and other forms of upper abdominal pain caused by muscle contractions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant contain a high concentration of apigenin, an antioxidant that helps to soothe and calm digestive issues.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers have been traditionally used to ease stomach discomfort, cramps, and spasms due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and leaves in early morning for maximum potency.
Weigh out 50 grams of the plant material and transfer it to a clean glass jar with a lid.
Fill a bottle with 100 milliliters of high-proof vodka and slowly pour it over the plant material while gently stirring.
Allow the mixture to macerate in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking daily, to allow for optimal extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

Flowers: Containing coumarins and sesquiterpenes, they have a calming effect on the digestive system, soothing cramps and discomfort in the upper abdomen.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers on a sunny day for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm, dry place or using a food dehydrator at 150°f for 2 hours.
Crush 20 grams of dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers into small pieces to release essential oils.
Mix crushed plant material with 100 proof vodka, covering it completely, and store in a glass jar for 6 weeks to infuse.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass bottle, discarding solids, and store in a dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tinctures helps with upper abdominal pain because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves in early morning or late afternoon for optimal potency.
Rinse the harvested leaves with filtered water and pat dry with a paper towel to remove excess moisture.
Combine one part dried thymus vulgaris leaves with two parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, filling it almost to the top.
Allow the mixture to steep at room temperature for four to six weeks, shaking the jar daily for optimal extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the liquid in a cool dark place.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to upper abdominal pain that are treated with herbal tinctures are digestive issues such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea. supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with upper abdominal pain is a blend of peppermint, ginger, and chamomile.
